headlines.title: Inside the South Korean mines that will provide U.S. with key metal for Iran War
headlines.description: In South Korea, an American company is mining for rare minerals used to make weapons and ammunition. Trade tensions with China have strained access to the mineral, tungsten, and the war with Iran is forcing the U.S. to find even more of it. NBC’s Janis Mackey Frayer reports from South Korea.

headlines.title: Trump Says He Told Negotiators 'Not to Rush Into' a Deal With Iran
headlines.description: The Trump administration and Iran both say progress is being made to end the war but there’s no deal yet. President Donald Trump says he’s told his representatives “not to rush into a deal,” while an Iran spokesman said, “No one can claim an agreement is about to be signed imminently.” The key terms at issue are Iran’s nuclear program and the fate of nearly 1,000lbs of highly enriched uranium, and control over the Strait of Hormuz. NBC’s Richard Engel reports for TODAY.

headlines.title: Risk of explosion has been ‘eliminated’ in California toxic chemical tank incident
headlines.description: Southern California officials said the risk of a devastating vapor explosion has been avoided in an incident involving a storage tank containing methyl methacrylate, a toxic chemical used to manufacture resins and plastics. NBC News' Steve Patterson reports on the subdued threat.

headlines.title: Jewish Democrats grapple with a changing party and Israel’s entrenched leadership
headlines.description: Onstage and on the sidelines of the recent Jewish Democratic Council of America Leadership Summit in Washington, prominent pro-Israel Democrats tiptoed into one of the biggest debates roiling their party

headlines.title: Crimes involving fake ICE agents are on the rise and becoming ‘much more aggressive and violent’
headlines.description: A Noticias Telemundo investigation documented a marked increase in cases of people posing as federal agents to rob, intimidate and even injure and rape immigrants.

headlines.title: 3 months, 21 dead gray whales: Why so many carcasses are washing up on Pacific shores
headlines.description: Dead, emaciated gray whales have been washing up on Washington state shores at a shocking pace over the last few months.

headlines.title: The 113+ absolute best Memorial Day sales worth shopping
headlines.description: Shop the best Memorial Day deals on mattresses, home appliances, beauty, tech and more from brands like Brooklinen, Ninja, Levoit, EltaMD and Apple.

headlines.title: Extended Interview: Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio
headlines.description: On the sidelines of the Trump-Xi Summit in Beijing, NBC News' Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio to discuss China policy, the war with Iran, the future of Cuba, and more.

headlines.title: What we know about the chemical tank incident in Southern California — and what questions still linger
headlines.description: What sent a chemical storage tank in Garden Grove, California, to the verge of either leaking or blowing up?

headlines.title: Spanish government confirms new case of hantavirus
headlines.description: The Spanish government on Monday said it has confirmed a new case of hantavirus connected to the cruise ship MV Hondius, which became the center of an outbreak that killed three people earlier this month

headlines.title: Ebola patients flee in attacks on DR Congo health facilities, hobbling response
headlines.description: Doctors operating on the front lines of the fight against Ebola in Congo are having to deal with attacks on their facilities and fleeing patients.

headlines.title: CBS backs off effort to stop sharing of Colbert’s surprise ‘Only in Monroe’ segment amid backlash
headlines.description: CBS said it had issued copyright notices for a recent episode featuring a surprise appearance by Stephen Colbert on the show “Only in Monroe” per its regular practice, but would stop issuing takedown notices pending further review.
